key_points: Middlebrook Hall corner rooms fit a 66-inch couch under the bed
Quad building has multi-semester complaints; students call it a nightmare
Single dorm assignments are common; mixed reactions from students
key_quotes: I have unironically done nothing this semester. I just sit in bed and stare into the void.
Every day for the last six semesters, I whisper those words to myself about the Quad building.
Awesome view! [re: Middlebrook river view]

key_points: Dinkytown bar scene is active but can attract aggressive drunk students
Campus preachers, cult recruiters, and scam charities are recurring annoyances
Wild turkey sightings and white squirrels are beloved campus traditions
key_quotes: 'I'm gonna go up to that guy over there with the satchel and call him a pussy' — overheard at McDonald's at 1am
Just an average night in Dinky.
GOBBLE GOBBLE MOTHER FUCKER. [re: campus turkeys]

key_points: Comstock dining hall mentioned but no strong praise or condemnation
Campus has Chick-fil-A; surrounding Dinkytown has solid food options
No major dining complaints surfaced, but not a highlight either
key_quotes: On my way to Comstock to get a healthy nutritious meal. (Salisbury steak and a single hot dog)
Applebee's is now a Chick-Fil-A?

key_points: Students openly struggling with dissociation, depression, and skipping classes
Professor response to a scared POC student's email was a wellness center auto-reply
Campus chaos around ICE, protests, and shootings is actively worsening student mental health
key_quotes: I have gone to like 2 classes all semester... I just cant bring myself to live my fucking life.
All I got in response was pretty much an automated email from their wellness center telling me to stop doomscrolling.
It's exhausting they expect us to show up and act like our city isn't under occupation.

key_points: Student health insurance is $1,795/semester and must be actively waived
Scholarships available including $100K offers to strong out-of-state applicants
Mandatory $200/year athletics fee added on top of tuition increases
key_quotes: It's *how much???* 10-15 years ago it was a couple hundred a semester. [re: health insurance]
I got into the University of Minnesota... with a $100,000 scholarship!
The most recent tuition increase is about twice the rate of inflation plus a $200 per student per year fee for athletics.

key_points: Chem 1061/1062 is widely dreaded; CS upperclassmen stress over career fairs
Professor lost a senior's final exam and tried to make them retake the course
ADA compliance changes eliminated PDF lecture notes, causing student panic
key_quotes: Teachers can't post notes anymore? Having the lecture notes was how I passed calc 2, I'm lowk panicking.
My professor gave me an incomplete and told me the registrar said I never took the final — which is a lie.
Wait until you get into 1062… it doesn't get better imo.

key_points: President Cunningham widely criticized for vague, spineless emails during crises
UMPD used LRAD riot weapons against student protesters; arrested bystanders
Professor was escorted out of a memorial for wearing an anti-ICE shirt
key_quotes: The NRA had a better response than President Cunningham. She would have been better off saying nothing at all.
She probably wrote those emails with an LLM. Cowardly and cheap.
I continue to be embarrassed for this institution. I am an alumna and worked at UMNTC for 25 years.

key_points: Minneapolis campus is beautiful but cold winters are a serious deterrent
Scooter and bike chaos on sidewalks causes injuries; traffic laws ignored
Green Line light rail stops on campus; people walk on tracks dangerously
key_quotes: The amount of people I see riding on electric scooters without a care in the world is insane. He hit me and gave me a bloody nose.
We've had to stop trains on the Green Line three times this morning because of people using the train tracks.
It makes me so lucky to go to this school [re: river view photo].

key_points: UMN alumni include Fortune 50 CEOs; McKesson CEO is a UMN grad
UMN ranked in top Nobel Prize-producing universities with 30 laureates
CS upperclassmen describe career fair grind as demoralizing
key_quotes: Going to career fair to find internship for people who don't give a shit about me.
University of Minnesota: #16 in Nobel Prize-producing universities.
I did this same exercise [Fortune 500 CEOs] years ago when picking a college.

key_points: Tuition rising faster than inflation with mandatory athletics fees added
Strong scholarship availability for high-stat out-of-state students
Football coach got $700K bonus while students absorb fee increases
key_quotes: The most recent tuition increase is about twice the rate of inflation plus a $200 per student per year fee was added for athletics (while giving the multimillion-dollar football coach a $700,000 bonus).
I got into the University of Minnesota with a $100,000 scholarship!
I sent a message pointing out that our family of graduates would no longer be donating or encouraging family members to attend.

key_points: Campus is politically charged, activist, and emotionally intense right now
Strong community loyalty from alumni despite frustration with leadership
Quirky campus culture: turkeys, white squirrels, bucket preachers are beloved
key_quotes: Row the Boat // Ski U Mah.
When I went to the U 20 years ago, we had a guy who stood on a bucket and yelled about Jesus for 4 years straight. I assume he is dead now and this is his son.
I am so sorry you are in this position. [student to student re: ICE fears]

key_points: **Safety**: ICE agents in plainclothes detained students near campus; POC students fear attending in person
**Administration**: University hosted ICE agents at on-campus hotel while silencing faculty critics at a memorial
**Campus Security**: UMPD deployed LRAD weapons on students, arrested bystanders, and allegedly assaulted a 67-year-old protester
key_quotes: A student was reported to have been taken this morning from a parking garage right next to Dinky.
She was on the picket line. She had to be hospitalized over multiple nights for brain bleeding. Not even a single apology from the university.
They are on campus and they are in street clothes. There are reports of them driving a blue Jeep Cherokee.

key_points: Gopher Grades website shows grade distributions for every professor and class
Health Sciences study rooms are bookable in advance — a major resource if you know about it
St. Paul campus is quieter and has its own distinct community feel
key_quotes: A group of students made a FOIA request for grade distributions for every class, professor, TA. The results are on gophergrades.com.
U card staff have been instructed on how to opt people in [to health coverage], please go visit, they were very helpful.
I'm a prof offering hybrid options for students who are worried. Email your profs to see if this is an option.
